=== SOURCE CODE (py) ===
'''
# This is a class to process IP Address, including validating, getting the octets and obtaining the binary representation of a valid IP address.

class IPAddress:
    def __init__(self, ip_address):
        """
        Initialize the IP address to the specified address
        :param ip_address:string
        """
        self.ip_address = ip_address


    def is_valid(self):
        """
        Judge whether the IP address is valid, that is, whether the IP address is composed of four Decimal digits separated by '.'. Each digit is greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 255
        :return: bool
        >>> ipaddress = IPAddress("10.10.10.10")
        >>> ipaddress.is_valid()
        True
        """


    def get_octets(self):
        """
        If the IP address is valid, the list of four decimal numbers separated by "." constituting the IP address is returned; otherwise, an empty list is returned
        :return: list
        >>> ipaddress = IPAddress("10.10.10.10")
        >>> ipaddress.get_octets()
        ["10", "10", "10", "10"]
        """


    def get_binary(self):
        """
        If the IP address is valid, return the binary form of the IP address; otherwise, return ''
        :return: string
        >>> ipaddress = IPAddress("10.10.10.10")
        >>> ipaddress.get_binary()
        "00001010.00001010.00001010.00001010"
        """
'''


class IPAddress:
    def __init__(self, ip_address):
        self.ip_address = ip_address

    def is_valid(self):
        octets = self.ip_address.split('.')
        if len(octets) != 4:
            return False
        for octet in octets:
            if not octet.isdigit() or int(octet) < 0 or int(octet) > 255:
                return False
        return True

    def get_octets(self):
        if self.is_valid():
            return self.ip_address.split('.')
        else:
            return []

    def get_binary(self):
        if self.is_valid():
            binary_octets = []
            for octet in self.get_octets():
                binary_octets.append(format(int(octet), '08b'))
            return '.'.join(binary_octets)
        else:
            return ''

      #include <vector>

      class IPAddress {
      private:
          std::string ip_address;

          // Helper function: split
          static std::vector<std::string> split(const std::string& s, char delimiter) {
              std::vector<std::string> tokens;
              std::string token;
              std::istringstream tokenStream(s);
              while (std::getline(tokenStream, token, delimiter)) {
                  tokens.push_back(token);
              }
              return tokens;
          }

      public:
          IPAddress(std::string ip_address) : ip_address(ip_address) {}

          bool is_valid() const {
              std::vector<std::string> tokens = split(ip_address, '.');
              if (tokens.size() != 4) {
                  return false;
              }
              for (const std::string& token : tokens) {
                  if (token.empty()) {
                      return false;
                  }
                  // Check each character is a digit
                  for (char c : token) {
                      if (!std::isdigit(static_cast<unsigned char>(c))) {
                          return false;
                      }
                  }
                  // Convert to unsigned long
                  try {
                      unsigned long value = std::stoul(token);
                      if (value > 255) {
                          return false;
                      }
                  } catch (...) {
                      return false;
                  }
              }
              return true;
          }

          std::vector<std::string> get_octets() const {
              if (is_valid()) {
                  return split(ip_address, '.');
              } else {
                  return {};
              }
          }

          std::string get_binary() const {
              if (!is_valid()) {
                  return "";
              }
              std::vector<std::string> octets = get_octets();
              std::vector<std::string> binary_octets;
              for (const auto& octet : octets) {
                  // We know octet is a string of digits and between 0 and 255
                  int value = std::stoi(octet);
                  binary_octets.push_back(std::bitset<8>(value).to_string());
              }
              // Join the binary_octets with '.'
              std::string result;
              for (size_t i = 0; i < binary_octets.size(); ++i) {
                  if (i != 0) {
                      result += '.';
                  }
                  result += binary_octets[i];
              }
              return result;
          }
      };

      #include <sstream>
      #include <cctype>
      #include <stdexcept>
      #include <bitset>

      class IPAddress {
      private:
          std::string ip_address;

          static std::vector<std::string> split(const std::string& s, char delimiter) {
              std::vector<std::string> tokens;
              std::string token;
              std::istringstream tokenStream(s);
              while (std::getline(tokenStream, token, delimiter)) {
                  tokens.push_back(token);
              }
              return tokens;
          }

      public:
          IPAddress(std::string ip_address) : ip_address(ip_address) {}

          bool is_valid() const {
              std::vector<std::string> tokens = split(ip_address, '.');
              if (tokens.size() != 4) {
                  return false;
              }
              for (const std::string& token : tokens) {
                  if (token.empty()) {
                      return false;
                  }
                  for (char c : token) {
                      // Note: std::isdigit may have issues with char signedness? Cast to unsigned char.
                      if (!std::isdigit(static_cast<unsigned char>(c))) {
                          return false;
                      }
                  }
                  try {
                      // Convert to unsigned long
                      size_t pos;
                      unsigned long value = std::stoul(token, &pos);
                      // Check that the entire string was consumed? Actually, we know it's digits so it should be. But what if stoul stops at non-digit? We already checked digits, so it should be safe.
                      // However, stoul might ignore trailing non-digit? But we have already checked every char is digit. So we can skip checking pos.
                      if (value > 255) {
                          return false;
                      }
                  } catch (const std::invalid_argument& e) {
                      return false;
                  } catch (const std::out_of_range& e) {
                      return false;
                  }
              }
              return true;
          }

          std::vector<std::string> get_octets() const {
              if (is_valid()) {
                  return split(ip_address, '.');
              } else {
                  return std::vector<std::string>();
              }
          }

          std::string get_binary() const {
              if (!is_valid()) {
                  return "";
              }
              std::vector<std::string> octets = get_octets();
              std::vector<std::string> binary_octets;
              for (const auto& octet : octets) {
                  int value = std::stoi(octet); // Safe because we validated
                  binary_octets.push_back(std::bitset<8>(value).to_string());
              }
              std::string result;
              for (size_t i = 0; i < binary_octets.size(); ++i) {
                  if (i != 0) {
                      result += '.';
                  }
                  result += binary_octets[i];
              }
              return result;
          }
      };

=== RAW ANSWER ===

To solve this problem, we need to translate a Python class that processes IP addresses into an equivalent C++ class. The class should validate IP addresses, retrieve their octets, and convert valid IP addresses into their binary representation. The behavior must remain identical to the original Python code, including handling edge cases and exceptions.

### Approach
1. **Problem Analysis**: The task involves creating a C++ class that mirrors the functionality of the given Python class. The class should:
   - Validate IP addresses to ensure they consist of four decimal numbers (each between 0 and 255) separated by dots.
   - Return the four octets as strings if the IP is valid; otherwise, return an empty list.
   - Convert a valid IP address into a binary string where each octet is represented as an 8-bit binary number separated by dots; return an empty string if the IP is invalid.

2. **Key Insights**:
   - **Validation**: The IP address must split into exactly four parts. Each part must be a non-empty string of digits, and the numeric value must be between 0 and 255.
   - **Octet Retrieval**: If valid, split the IP string into its four components; otherwise, return an empty vector.
   - **Binary Conversion**: For each valid octet, convert it to an 8-bit binary string with leading zeros and join them with dots.

3. **Algorithm Selection**:
   - **Splitting Strings**: Use `std::getline` with a string stream to split the IP string by dots.
   - **Validation**: Check each split token for being non-empty, all digits, and within the 0-255 range using `std::stoul` for conversion and range checking.
   - **Binary Conversion**: Utilize `std::bitset<8>` to convert each octet to an 8-bit binary string.

4. **Complexity Analysis**:
   - **Splitting**: O(n) where n is the length of the IP string.
   - **Validation**: O(n) per token, with each token processed in linear time.
   - **Binary Conversion**: O(1) per octet, as each conversion is fixed-time due to the 8-bit constraint.

### Solution Code
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <sstream>
#include <cctype>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <bitset>

class IPAddress {
private:
    std::string ip_address;

    static std::vector<std::string> split(const std::string& s, char delimiter) {
        std::vector<std::string> tokens;
        std::string token;
        std::istringstream tokenStream(s);
        while (std::getline(tokenStream, token, delimiter)) {
            tokens.push_back(token);
        }
        return tokens;
    }

public:
    IPAddress(std::string ip_address) : ip_address(ip_address) {}

    bool is_valid() const {
        std::vector<std::string> tokens = split(ip_address, '.');
        if (tokens.size() != 4) {
            return false;
        }
        for (const std::string& token : tokens) {
            if (token.empty()) {
                return false;
            }
            for (char c : token) {
                if (!std::isdigit(static_cast<unsigned char>(c))) {
                    return false;
                }
            }
            try {
                size_t pos;
                unsigned long value = std::stoul(token, &pos);
                if (value > 255) {
                    return false;
                }
            } catch (const std::invalid_argument& e) {
                return false;
            } catch (const std::out_of_range& e) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }

    std::vector<std::string> get_octets() const {
        if (is_valid()) {
            return split(ip_address, '.');
        } else {
            return std::vector<std::string>();
        }
    }

    std::string get_binary() const {
        if (!is_valid()) {
            return "";
        }
        std::vector<std::string> octets = get_octets();
        std::vector<std::string> binary_octets;
        for (const auto& octet : octets) {
            int value = std::stoi(octet);
            binary_octets.push_back(std::bitset<8>(value).to_string());
        }
        std::string result;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < binary_octets.size(); ++i) {
            if (i != 0) {
                result += '.';
            }
            result += binary_octets[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```

### Explanation
- **Splitting the IP String**: The `split` function uses `std::getline` to divide the IP string into tokens separated by dots.
- **Validation**: The `is_valid` method checks if there are exactly four tokens. Each token is verified to be non-empty, composed solely of digits, and within the 0-255 range using `std::stoul` for conversion and range checks.
- **Octet Retrieval**: The `get_octets` method returns the split tokens if valid; otherwise, it returns an empty vector.
- **Binary Conversion**: The `get_binary` method processes each valid octet by converting it to an integer, then to an 8-bit binary string using `std::bitset<8>`, and joins them with dots to form the final binary representation. If the IP is invalid, it returns an empty string.

This approach ensures that the C++ implementation behaves identically to the original Python code, handling all edge cases and maintaining the same input-output expectations.

#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <sstream>
#include <cctype>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <bitset>

class IPAddress {
private:
    std::string ip_address;

    static std::vector<std::string> split(const std::string& s, char delimiter) {
        std::vector<std::string> tokens;
        std::string token;
        std::istringstream tokenStream(s);
        while (std::getline(tokenStream, token, delimiter)) {
            tokens.push_back(token);
        }
        return tokens;
    }

public:
    IPAddress(std::string ip_address) : ip_address(ip_address) {}

    bool is_valid() const {
        std::vector<std::string> tokens = split(ip_address, '.');
        if (tokens.size() != 4) {
            return false;
        }
        for (const std::string& token : tokens) {
            if (token.empty()) {
                return false;
            }
            for (char c : token) {
                if (!std::isdigit(static_cast<unsigned char>(c))) {
                    return false;
                }
            }
            try {
                size_t pos;
                unsigned long value = std::stoul(token, &pos);
                if (value > 255) {
                    return false;
                }
            } catch (const std::invalid_argument& e) {
                return false;
            } catch (const std::out_of_range& e) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }

    std::vector<std::string> get_octets() const {
        if (is_valid()) {
            return split(ip_address, '.');
        } else {
            return std::vector<std::string>();
        }
    }

    std::string get_binary() const {
        if (!is_valid()) {
            return "";
        }
        std::vector<std::string> octets = get_octets();
        std::vector<std::string> binary_octets;
        for (const auto& octet : octets) {
            int value = std::stoi(octet);
            binary_octets.push_back(std::bitset<8>(value).to_string());
        }
        std::string result;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < binary_octets.size(); ++i) {
            if (i != 0) {
                result += '.';
            }
            result += binary_octets[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
};
